title: Sendinblue Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict
meta_description: Is Sendinblue worth it in 2025? We break down pricing, features, pros and cons so you can decide.
h1: Sendinblue Review 2025: Everything You Need to Know
intro: Sendinblue is now brevo — the all-in-one crm suite that positions itself for transactional email and SMS campaigns. Founded in 2012, it has become one of the most recognised platforms in the email marketing space. In this review we cover what it does well, where it falls short, and who should actually use it.
verdict: Sendinblue is a solid choice for transactional email and SMS campaigns. Its free tier is genuinely useful. The main trade-off is between its particular strengths and the limitations that make alternatives worth considering for some users.
pros: Good fit for transactional email and SMS campaigns
cons: Can get expensive at higher subscriber counts
Some features locked to higher tiers
pricing_summary: Sendinblue offers a free plan, with paid plans from $25/month. Pricing typically scales with subscriber count.
best_for_detail: Sendinblue works best for transactional email and SMS campaigns. If you fit that profile, the feature set aligns well. If you need something different, check the alternatives section.

faq.a: Yes, Sendinblue has a free plan supporting up to 100000 subscribers. This makes it accessible for new senders testing the platform.

faq.q: What is Sendinblue best used for?
faq.a: Sendinblue is best for transactional email and SMS campaigns. Its standout features include 300 free emails/day, SMS, WhatsApp, which suit this use case well.
Sendinblue is generally beginner-friendly. The interface is straightforward for basic campaigns, though advanced automation features have a learning curve.

faq.q: Does Sendinblue have an affiliate program?
faq.a: Yes, Sendinblue runs an affiliate program paying 5 EUR free + 100 EUR paid. This is worth considering if you plan to recommend the platform to your audience.
